data.title: CrowdStrike (CRWD) Expands Strategic Collaborations with Intel, IBM amid AI Push
data.description:
Cybersecurity company CrowdStrike (CRWD) announced expanded partnerships with tech giant IBM (IBM) and chipmaker Intel (INTC) to ensure safety amid growing adoption of artificial intelligence (AI). The company extended its multi-year collaboration with Intel to optimize its Falcon platform for Intel-powered AI PCs. Meanwhile, CrowdStrike expanded its strategic collaboration with IBM to accelerate agentic SOC (Security Operations Center) transformation.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade IBM with leverage
  • New trading tool for IBM bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade IBM

CRWD stock has declined 18% so far in 2026 due to macro headwinds and concerns that generative AI tools could disrupt traditional software companies. Concerns about AI disruption increased after Anthropic unveiled its Claude Code Security.

CrowdStrike Expands Partnership with Intel

Under the extended partnership with Intel, CrowdStrike's Falcon platform will offer high-performance security at enterprise scale by leveraging Intel Threat Detection Technology and Intel vPro's hardware-assisted recovery.

By combining CRWD's Falcon platform with Intel's AI PC architectures, the two companies intend to ensure real-time security and data protection and enhance their co-engineering pipeline to further boost AI-powered security at the edge.

CRWD Extends Deal with IBM

Meanwhile, CrowdStrike's expanded partnership with IBM involves integrating the cybersecurity company's Charlotte AI platform with IBM's Autonomous Threat Operations Machine (ATOM), the tech giant's autonomous SOC orchestration engine, to enable machine-speed investigation and containment.

The deal will also integrate the Falcon platform into IBM Consulting's managed Threat Detection and Response services and global X-Force Cyber Range to offer cyber crisis simulations and help enterprises prepare for emerging threats.

Overall, the partnership combines the SOC, IBM ATOM, and Charlotte AI to assess detections across endpoint, identity, and cloud environments.

Is CRWD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

With 27 Buys and nine Holds, CrowdStrike scores a Strong Buy consensus rating. The average CRWD stock price target of $484.33 indicates 25.5% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: META Stock Slides after Losing Landmark Addiction Case—DA Davidson Calls It a 'Setback'
data.description:
Shares of Meta Platforms (META) were down by 0.47% in after-hours trading on Wednesday after the company lost a landmark U.S. lawsuit over social media addiction. A Los Angeles jury found Meta and Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google negligent for designing platforms that harm young users. D.A. Davidson's four-star-rated tech analyst Gil Luria called the verdict a "setback" for these tech giants.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Luria added that the verdict could stretch through future cases and appeals, but it may eventually push these companies to add consumer protections, which could slow growth. Luria has a Buy rating on META stock with a price target of $850, implying an upside of over 40% from current levels.

Backdrop to the Case

The case involves Kaley, a 20-year-old who became addicted to Instagram and YouTube as a minor due to features like infinite scroll. The jury found both companies "negligent" in app design and failing to warn users of potential harms.

This marks Meta's second major loss this week. On March 24, a New Mexico jury found the company guilty of hiding information about child sexual exploitation risks and the harmful effects of its platforms on children's mental health. The jury fined Meta $375 million for violating safety law.

Other defendants, TikTok and Snap (SNAP), settled on confidential terms before the six-week trial began.

Meta Liable for $4.2M in Damages

A Los Angeles jury ordered Meta to pay $4.2 million and Google $1.8 million. While these amounts are small for tech giants spending over $100 billion annually, the real impact could be much larger.

However, the real significance isn't just the money. The ruling could set a legal precedent, opening the door for hundreds of similar lawsuits, forcing changes to app designs, and increasing regulatory and investor pressure. In other words, the long-term effects could be far more costly than the initial payout.

Is META Stock a Good Buy Now?

While the verdict may affect short-term momentum, the long-term outlook for Meta remains stable. Turning to Wall Street, Meta's shares have a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks, based on 40 Buys and five Holds assigned over the last three months. Furthermore, the average META price target of $865.58 indicates a 45.5% growth potential from its current price.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Powers the AI Boom — Bullish despite the Pullback
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) is a critical but underappreciated force behind the artificial intelligence (AI) buildout, powering key layers of the infrastructure stack. While the stock has fallen more than 24% from its December 2025 highs, it is still up more than 62% over the past 12 months. Moreover, the company's role across custom AI silicon, high-speed networking, and infrastructure software continues to deepen as hyperscalers expand. I remain bullish as Broadcom's role in the AI boom looks more durable and diversified than the recent pullback suggests, creating a good opportunity.

data.title: Shareholders Sue Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Over Alleged Securities Fraud
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) shareholders have sued the AI server maker, alleging that the company committed securities fraud by hiding its reliance on sales to China, which resulted in a violation of U.S. export laws, Reuters reported.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

The lawsuit follows criminal charges against Yih-Shyan Liaw, a co-founder and director; Ruei-Tsang Chang, a sales manager in Taiwan; ​and Ting-Wei Sun, a contractor, for smuggling servers powered by Nvidia (NVDA) chips into China. SMCI stock plunged 33% on March 20 in reaction to the news. The company issued a statement saying that it has not been named a defendant by the Department of Justice and that it has been fully cooperating with the government's investigation.

SMCI Faces Class Action Lawsuit

The class action lawsuit filed by shareholders in San Francisco federal court accuses Super Micro Computer of overstating its business prospects and inflating its stock price by deliberately concealing that a significant portion of its server sales was to companies in China.

The lawsuit also accuses SMCI of failing to disclose that its sales of servers to companies in China violated export laws. It also notes material weaknesses in the company's controls to ensure compliance with export control laws and regulations.

The lawsuit seeks unspecified damages for SMCI investors who purchased the stock between April 30, 2024, and March 19, 2026.

Analysts' Reactions

Several analysts have lowered their price targets for SMCI stock following the news of the indictment of three individuals associated with the company.

On Wednesday, Rosenblatt Securities analyst Kevin Cassidy lowered the price target for Super Micro Computer stock to $32 from $50, while reiterating a Buy rating. The 5-star analyst thinks that the controversy around Liaw and the U.S. Attorney's indictment alleging export-control violations puts "a dark cloud over what would have been an exciting product announcement."

While Cassidy remains confident about SMCI and its "sound" order backlog, he expects shares to be under pressure until the investigation is completed.

Meanwhile, Bank of America analyst Ruplu Bhattacharya cut his price target for SMCI stock to $24 from $34 and reaffirmed a Sell rating. The 4-star analyst contends that, while the company is not named as a defendant in the indictment of co-founder Liaw, it is another negative for Super Micro's brand. Bhattacharya expressed concerns that the indictment could result in some suppliers restricting SMCI's access to components, customers pausing orders or awarding contracts to rivals, and further regulatory scrutiny.

Is SMCI a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on Super Micro Computer stock based on eight Holds, three Buys, and three Sell recommendations. The average SMCI price target of $31.70 indicates 32% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Stock Market Review Today: SPY, QQQ Rebound as U.S.-Iran Exchange Ceasefire Terms; Trump Reschedules Xi Summit
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) in the green on Wednesday as optimism over a U.S.-Iran ceasefire pushed investor sentiment higher.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • The U.S. and Iran have each outlined their conditions to end the war, although major disagreements remain on both sides.
  • The U.S. called on Iran to cease uranium enrichment, end its nuclear weapons program, and reopen the Strait of Hormuz as a free maritime corridor. Iran's proposal includes war reparations, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and an end to "aggression and assassinations" by the U.S. and Israel.
  • President Trump rescheduled his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping to May 14-15. The meeting, originally scheduled for March 31 to April 2, was pushed back due to the war in Iran.
  • Trump signed an executive order to create the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST). The council will advise the administration on developments in AI, quantum computing, and biotechnology and will include the CEOs of Nvidia (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), and Google (GOOGL), among others.
  • A jury ruled that Meta and Google are responsible for creating platforms that pose risks to teenagers.
  • AMD (AMD) surged 7% following reports that the company would hike CPU prices by an average of 10%-15%.
  • UBS lowered its Microsoft (MSFT) price target to $510 from $600, citing competition in the AI space and poor sentiment surrounding the company's M365 and Copilot services.
  • BlackRock (BLK) CEO Larry Fink warned of a "stark and steep recession" if oil pushes above $150.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

The materials sector was the best-performing group on Wednesday as hopes for a U.S.-Iran ceasefire contributed to lower oil prices. Cheaper oil reduces production costs for metals, chemicals, and other materials companies and helps support stronger profit margins for the sector.

Several stocks led the materials sector's gains, including:

  • International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF)
  • Amcor (AMCR)
  • PPG Industries (PPG)
  • Dow Inc. (DOW)
  • Mosaic Company (MOS)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the energy sector faced the largest drawdown. Energy stocks often move inversely with materials stocks when oil rises or falls significantly. Higher oil prices boost energy profits but raise costs for materials companies, while lower oil prices reduce energy revenues and improve margins for materials firms.

Notable energy stocks trading lower include:

  • Valero Energy (VLO)
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM)
  • Phillips 66 (PSX)
  • Baker Hughes (BKR)
  • Chevron (CVX)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Steady after Middle East Peace Hopes Lift the Market
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were flat on Wednesday evening after a strong regular session, aided by growing optimism over potential progress toward peace in the Middle East. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.05%, 0.06%, and 0.04%, respectively, at 6:34 p.m. EDT on March 25.

Major stock indices gained in the regular trading session amid cautious optimism about a potential ceasefire in the Middle East and a drop in energy prices. The Nasdaq, the Dow, and the S&P 500 rose 0.8%, 0.7%, and 0.5%, respectively.

Looking ahead, traders await the latest initial jobless claims for the week ending March 21. On the earnings front, Blink Charging (BLNK) and Pony AI (PONY) are scheduled to report quarterly numbers tomorrow.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.description:

data.title: JEF Earnings: Jefferies Financial Group Posts Record Revenue amid Takeover Speculation
data.description:
Jefferies Financial Group (JEF) has reported solid financial results on a day when media reports surfaced that the Wall Street investment bank is a takeover target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jefferies posted record revenues, although it missed analysts' profit expectations. The boutique investment bank reported earnings per share (EPS) of $0.70, which fell short of the consensus forecast of $0.95. Revenue in the period totaled $2.02 billion, which matched analyst estimates.

Sales were up 27% year-over-year and were a record for the company. The latest results included a $36 million after-tax write-down of goodwill related to the announced sale of Tessellis and $17 million in losses from private credit firms Market Financial Solutions and First Brands.

The firm said that its investment banking revenues reached a record $1.02 billion during the quarter, up 45% from a year earlier and driven by improved underwriting performance. Capital markets revenues totaled $779 million, up 12% from a year ago, with equities posting record revenue of $558 million. Fixed income revenues declined to $220 million from $289 million a year earlier.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: GameStop's Q4 Earnings Prove the Rise of PC Gaming Is Bad for GME Stock
data.description:
GameStop (GME) stock could be in trouble soon, as a shift in gaming trends could leave the video game retailer with a shrinking customer base. The company reported its Q4 results after markets closed on Tuesday, and revenue for the holiday quarter fell 10.57% year-over-year to $1.1 billion. A major reason for this revenue drop was the game industry's shift away from physical games.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

PC gaming has largely been digital for over a decade now, as platforms such as Valve's Steam and the Epic Games Store offer direct digital sales to consumers. On top of that, PC gaming is growing. Some experts predict that PC gaming revenue will surpass console gaming revenue as early as 2028. This shift would further harm physical video game retailers, such as GameStop.

Finally, console gaming has also started to turn away from physical games. Instead, video game publishers have pushed for digital sales, which return bigger profits as no physical media has to be created. Adding to that trend are major companies offering subscription services, including Microsoft's (MSFT) Xbox Game Pass, Sony's (SONY) PlayStation Plus, and Nintendo's (NTDOF) Switch Online services. All of these offer games to subscribers without the need for a physical sale.

What Does This Mean for GameStop Stock?

If the shift to digital gaming continues to gain momentum, it would mean ongoing drops in revenue from physical game sales. This could spell trouble for GameStop, as video game sales make up a large portion of its revenue.

This isn't to say that GameStop is unaware of the issue. The retailer has been expanding into other avenues of pop culture to help prop up its sales. That includes buying and selling individual trading cards, such as Pokémon, Dragon Ball Super, Disney (DIS) Lorcana, Hasbro's (HAS) Magic: The Gathering, and sports cards. However, it only deals in graded cards, which limits its audience to collectors and not those who play trading card games.

Is GameStop Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of GameStop is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Spark rates GME stock as Neutral with a $23.50 price target. It cites "improving profitability and cash flow, supported by disciplined cost control, but tempered by ongoing revenue declines and a more risk-prone balance-sheet profile" as reasons for this stance.
data.author: William White
